title: NOC 30010 – Managers in Health Care | GTR Immigration
description: Health Care Managers are in strong demand across Canada’s healthcare system, particularly as hospitals, long-term care facilities, and specialty clinics expand services to meet growing population needs. Classified under NOC 30010 (TEER 0 – Management Occupation), this role aligns well with:
common_job_titles: Mental health residential care program manager
duties: Plan, organize, direct, and evaluate health service delivery
Implement new healthcare programs and equipment upgrades
Represent departments in meetings with government officials and stakeholders
Ensure compliance with healthcare regulations and standards
employment_requirements: Professional education in the relevant health disciplinePhysician-specialist training (for surgical/clinical administration roles)Nursing, physiotherapy, laboratory, or dietetics qualifications (for department leadership roles)
Nursing, physiotherapy, laboratory, or dietetics qualifications (for department leadership roles)
Several years of professional and supervisory experience
Professional certification in the relevant healthcare field
In many provinces, licensing by provincial regulatory authorities
job_outlook: Although competitive, this occupation remains attractive—especially for candidates with:
pathways: Direct PR opportunities in healthcare streams
Long-term leadership growth into executive roles
additional_information: Healthcare administrators are being recruited by:
Hospitals & acute-care centersLong-term care & nursing homesMedical clinics & specialty practicesRehabilitation & mental health facilitiesHome-care organizations
Typical annual salaries: $50,100–$133,900 CAD, depending on province, specialty, and seniority.
Senior leadership roles—particularly in nursing, clinical operations, and rehabilitation—often command salaries above the national median.
